title: Why Many Smokers Are Wrong About Vaping Risks
subtitle: New research shows that public confusion about e-cigarettes is preventing smokers from quitting a deadly habit.

summary.en: A new study reveals that more than half of UK smokers wrongly believe vaping is as dangerous as smoking cigarettes. Experts warn that this misunderstanding stops people from switching to a less harmful alternative. Research from King’s College London confirms that vaping carries only a fraction of the risk of tobacco. Many smokers are now choosing to return to cigarettes instead of using vapes to quit. Health officials are calling for better public education to correct these false beliefs.
summary.zh: 一項新研究顯示，超過半數的英國吸菸者錯誤地認為電子菸與吸食紙菸同樣危險。專家警告，這種誤解阻礙了人們轉向危害較小的替代品。倫敦國王學院的研究證實，電子菸的風險僅為菸草的一小部分。目前許多吸菸者選擇回頭吸食紙菸，而非使用電子菸來戒菸。衛生官員呼籲加強公眾教育，以導正這些錯誤觀念。

paragraphs.en: A growing number of smokers in the UK are turning away from vaping because they wrongly believe it is just as dangerous as smoking traditional cigarettes. New data released by Action on Smoking and Health (Ash) shows that this confusion is becoming a serious public health problem. According to the research, more than half of adult smokers now believe that vaping is as harmful or even more harmful than smoking tobacco. This is a significant change from a decade ago, when only about a quarter of adults held this view.
paragraphs.zh: 越來越多英國吸菸者開始排斥電子菸，因為他們誤以為電子菸與傳統紙菸同樣危險。由「吸菸與健康行動組織 (Action on Smoking and Health, Ash)」發布的最新數據顯示，這種認知混淆正演變成嚴重的公共衛生問題。根據該研究，超過半數的成年吸菸者現在認為，使用電子菸與吸食菸草一樣有害，甚至危害更大。這與十年前的情況相比有顯著差異，當時僅約四分之一的成年人持有這種觀點。

paragraphs.en: This trend is worrying experts who study tobacco use. According to The Guardian Health, the research found that these false beliefs are making it harder for people to quit smoking. When smokers think that vaping offers no health benefit, they are less likely to try it as a way to stop smoking. Even more concerning, some people who were already using vapes have decided to stop and return to smoking cigarettes. Experts note that this is a dangerous step backward for their health.
paragraphs.zh: 這種趨勢令研究菸草使用的專家感到憂心。根據《衛報健康版 (The Guardian Health)》報導，研究發現這些錯誤觀念正導致人們更難戒菸。當吸菸者認為電子菸對健康沒有益處時，他們嘗試以電子菸作為戒菸工具的意願就會降低。更令人擔憂的是，部分原本使用電子菸的人決定停止使用並回頭吸食紙菸。專家指出，這對他們的健康而言是危險的倒退。

paragraphs.en: Scientific evidence shows that while vaping is not completely risk-free, it is far less dangerous than smoking. Tobacco smoke contains thousands of chemicals, including poisonous gases and substances that cause cancer. A major review by King’s College London found that e-cigarettes represent only a small fraction of the risk compared to tobacco. People who vape are exposed to significantly fewer harmful substances than those who smoke cigarettes.
paragraphs.zh: 科學證據顯示，雖然電子菸並非完全沒有風險，但其危險程度遠低於吸菸。菸草燃燒後的煙霧含有數千種化學物質，包括有毒氣體及致癌物質。倫敦國王學院 (King’s College London) 的一項大型審查研究發現，電子菸帶來的風險僅是菸草的一小部分。相較於吸食紙菸者，使用電子菸的人所接觸到的有害物質顯著較少。

paragraphs.en: Why has public opinion changed so much? Prof Jamie Brown, director of the UCL Tobacco and Alcohol Research Group, explained that it is difficult to identify one single cause. However, he noted that many scientific studies report potential health risks of vaping without comparing them to the much higher risks of smoking. When these studies appear in the news, the media often focuses on the dangers of vaping while failing to mention how much worse cigarettes are. As a result, the public is left with a distorted view of the facts.
paragraphs.zh: 為何大眾觀感會有如此巨大的轉變？倫敦大學學院 (UCL) 菸酒研究小組主任布朗教授 (Prof Jamie Brown) 解釋，很難歸咎於單一原因。然而他指出，許多科學研究在報導電子菸的潛在健康風險時，並未將其與吸菸所帶來的更高風險進行比較。當這些研究登上新聞時，媒體往往聚焦於電子菸的危害，卻忽略提及紙菸的危害程度嚴重得多。結果導致大眾對事實產生了扭曲的認知。

paragraphs.en: Hazel Cheeseman, the chief executive of Ash, said that these misconceptions have real-world consequences. She pointed out that if smokers do not understand the relative safety of vapes, they lose an effective tool for quitting. Prof Peter Hajek, director of the Health and Lifestyle Research Unit at Queen Mary University of London, added that some anti-smoking advocates may be making the problem worse. He suggested that some people believe it is acceptable to exaggerate the harms of vaping if it helps discourage nicotine use in general. However, he warned that this approach effectively closes the door to a relatively easy way for smokers to escape a deadly habit.
paragraphs.zh: 「吸菸與健康行動組織」執行長奇斯曼 (Hazel Cheeseman) 表示，這些誤解會造成現實層面的後果。她指出，如果吸菸者不了解電子菸的相對安全性，他們就失去了一項有效的戒菸工具。倫敦瑪麗皇后大學 (Queen Mary University of London) 健康與生活型態研究單位主任哈傑克教授 (Prof Peter Hajek) 補充說，部分反菸倡議者可能加劇了這個問題。他認為有些人覺得，若誇大電子菸的危害有助於抑制整體尼古丁的使用，那是可以接受的。但他警告，這種做法實際上是斷絕了吸菸者擺脫致命習慣的一條相對容易的途徑。

paragraphs.en: The survey, which was conducted by YouGov for Ash, included more than 13,000 adults across Great Britain. The results showed that 52% of current smokers believe vaping is as harmful or more harmful than smoking. This number is even higher among smokers who have never tried vaping, reaching 61%. Fewer than one-third of smokers correctly understood that vaping is less harmful than smoking. Perhaps most surprisingly, the survey found that nearly one-fifth of people who tried to quit vaping did so by switching to cigarettes.
paragraphs.zh: 這項由輿觀調查公司 (YouGov) 為「吸菸與健康行動組織」所進行的調查，涵蓋了全英國超過一萬三千名成年人。結果顯示，52% 的現任吸菸者認為電子菸與吸菸一樣有害或危害更大。在從未嘗試過電子菸的吸菸者中，此比例更高達 61%。僅不到三分之一的吸菸者正確理解電子菸的危害低於紙菸。最令人驚訝的或許是，調查發現近五分之一嘗試戒除電子菸的人，是透過改吸紙菸來達成。

paragraphs.en: This issue is becoming more urgent as the government prepares to introduce new rules for vaping products. As ministers work on the tobacco and vapes bill, experts are calling for clearer communication to the public. Prof Brown emphasized that two out of three smokers will eventually die from a smoking-related illness if they do not quit. Because e-cigarettes are a proven method to help people stop smoking, he argued that correcting these false beliefs is a top priority for public health. Without clear and accurate information, many smokers may continue to use the most dangerous products, unaware that a safer, effective alternative is available to them.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著政府準備針對電子菸產品引入新規定，此議題變得更加迫切。在部長們研擬菸草與電子菸法案的同時，專家呼籲應對大眾進行更清晰的溝通。布朗教授強調，若不戒菸，三分之二的吸菸者最終會死於與吸菸相關的疾病。由於電子菸已被證實是協助人們戒菸的有效方法，他主張糾正這些錯誤觀念是公共衛生的首要任務。若缺乏清晰且準確的資訊，許多吸菸者可能會繼續使用最危險的產品，而不知道其實有更安全、有效的替代方案可供選擇。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To stop using or being interested in something.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 不再使用、放棄、轉而遠離某事物。
keyTerms.noteZh: 這裡指戒除或停止使用電子煙。文中：A growing number of smokers in the UK are turning away from vaping because they wrongly believe it is just as dangerous as smoking traditional cigarettes.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A return to a worse situation or a lower level of progress.
keyTerms.noteZh: 用「後退的一步」比喻健康狀況惡化。文中：Experts note that this is a dangerous step backward for their health.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A small part or amount of something.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見於數學（分數），這裡指風險程度很小。文中：A major review by King’s College London found that e-cigarettes represent only a small fraction of the risk compared to tobacco.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To make it impossible for something to happen or for someone to achieve something.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 斷絕……的機會、關閉……的大門。
keyTerms.noteZh: 比喻性用法，指阻礙了某種可能性。文中：However, he warned that this approach effectively closes the door to a relatively easy way for smokers to escape a deadly habit.

sources.name: The Guardian Health
sources.title: Majority of UK smokers wrongly believe vaping is as harmful as cigarettes, experts find

previousArticles.title: Why Many Smokers Are Wrong About Vaping Risks
previousArticles.summaryEn: A new study reveals that over half of UK smokers wrongly believe vaping is as dangerous as smoking cigarettes. Experts warn that this misunderstanding stops people from switching to a less harmful alternative. Research from King’s College London confirms that vapes expose users to far fewer toxic substances than tobacco. Media coverage focusing on vaping risks without comparing them to smoking is identified as a major cause of this confusion. Health officials are now calling for better public information to help smokers quit successfully.

previousArticles.title: New Research Highlights Risks of Long Periods of Sitting
previousArticles.summaryEn: A new study involving over 90,000 people suggests that sitting for more than 30 minutes at a time is linked to a higher risk of cancer death. Researchers found that every extra hour of continuous inactivity increases this risk by 10%. However, the study also shows that replacing sedentary time with even light physical activity can be protective. Experts emphasize that while the findings are significant, the study is observational and cannot prove that sitting directly causes cancer. Future research will aim to develop more personalized health strategies for different individuals.

previousArticles.title: NHS Expands AI Tools to Improve Patient Care and Reduce Wait Times
previousArticles.summaryEn: The NHS is launching a new AI tool on its mobile app to help patients find the right medical services. This system will triage patients, directing them to pharmacies or A&E if they do not need a GP appointment. The project is part of a large £10bn investment to update technology across the health service. While officials believe this will save time, some health experts are concerned about privacy and the lack of a clear long-term strategy. The government hopes these changes will eventually end the difficult morning rush for same-day appointments.

quiz.question: According to the Ash survey, what is the percentage of smokers who have never tried vaping that believe it is as harmful as or more harmful than smoking?
quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 D。根據第六段，調查顯示在從未嘗試過電子菸的吸菸者中，認為其與傳統香菸一樣有害或更甚的比例高達 61%。選項 A（19%）是指嘗試戒掉電子菸後改抽香菸的比例；選項 B（小於三分之一）是指正確理解電子菸危害較小的人數比例；選項 C（52%）則是所有現任吸菸者對電子菸危害認知的平均值，而非針對未嘗試過者的數據。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the role of media and scientific reporting in the current public health crisis?

options.A: The media is intentionally trying to promote tobacco sales to boost the economy.
options.B: Scientific studies are being falsified to make vaping look more dangerous than it is.
options.C: The lack of context in health reporting creates a misleading perception of relative risk.
options.D: Journalists are the primary group responsible for the decline in vaping among young people.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第四段，Prof Jamie Brown 指出許多科學研究僅報導電子菸的潛在風險，卻未將其與吸菸的高風險進行比較，媒體在報導時也忽略了此對比，導致大眾產生扭曲的認知。選項 A 無法由文中推論；選項 B 錯誤，文中並未指控科學數據造假，而是指控「缺乏比較」；選項 D 錯誤，文中討論的是整體大眾的認知，並未特別強調年輕族群。

quiz.question: In the fifth paragraph, what does the phrase 'closes the door' mean in the context of Prof Peter Hajek’s warning?

options.A: To physically lock an entrance to a room.
options.B: To prevent someone from accessing a helpful opportunity.
options.C: To finish a conversation or stop a debate.
options.D: To hide the truth from the general public.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。在此語境下，「closes the door」是一種隱喻，意指誇大電子菸危害的行為，實際上剝奪了吸菸者利用電子菸作為戒菸工具的機會。選項 A 是字面意思，不符合語境；選項 C 雖然在某些情境下適用，但在此處是指剝奪選擇權而非結束對話；選項 D 雖然與「隱瞞」相關，但原文強調的是讓吸菸者「無法進入」或「無法使用」戒菸途徑，而非單純隱瞞資訊。

quiz.question: What is the primary message of the article regarding public perception of vaping?

options.A: Vaping is completely safe, and the government should stop regulating it.
options.B: Smokers are choosing to vape more frequently due to new health information.
options.C: Misinformation about the relative safety of vaping is hindering efforts to reduce smoking.
options.D: Scientists and the media are working together to promote vaping as a healthy habit.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。本文的核心在於探討大眾對電子菸危害的錯誤認知（認為其與香菸一樣危險），這種誤解導致吸菸者不願嘗試以電子菸戒菸，甚至回頭抽香菸，這對公共衛生造成了嚴重阻礙。選項 A 與文意不符，文中提到電子菸並非完全無風險；選項 B 與事實相反，吸菸者正因為誤解而遠離電子菸；選項 D 錯誤，文中提到媒體與專家的報導方式反而加劇了誤解，並非推廣電子菸為健康習慣。
